description_long: This object consists of several rectangular concrete blocks, each with two large hollow cavities running through their length, typical of cinder or concrete masonry units. The blocks are stacked and scattered in a loosely arranged pile, with some blocks lying flat and others propped at angles. The surfaces are matte and slightly rough, with visible edges and corners that are mostly sharp but show minor chipping. The color is a uniform light gray, characteristic of standard concrete, and there are no visible markings or engravings. The blocks appear solid and heavy, suitable for use as barriers or construction elements in urban environments. The hollow cores are visible from several angles, indicating the blocks are not fully solid and would be lighter than a solid stone of the same size.
description: A pile of light gray, hollow concrete blocks with rough, matte surfaces and visible cavities, arranged in a loose stack. The blocks show minor chipping and are typical of urban construction or barrier use.
description_view_0: Several concrete blocks are stacked and scattered, with hollow cavities visible in some blocks and a prominent block resting on top.
description_view_1: The view highlights the hollow cores of two blocks facing forward, with other blocks partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: The rear of the pile is visible, showing the solid ends of some blocks and the hollow openings of others, with a block standing upright in the center.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the pile, emphasizing the hollow cavities of the blocks and the rough, matte texture of the surfaces.
attribute_location_context: urban construction site or roadside barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping on edges and corners, no significant weathering or discoloration visible.
